generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a blocked nose and a foul smell that would not quit for months.

Then the X-ray loaded 😳

And something solid was sitting high up inside the nose where nothing should be.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What is the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Tooth in the nasal cavity

👀 What you see:
A real tooth growing inside the nose. A fully formed tooth with a crown and a root sitting high in the nasal cavity well above where any tooth belongs.

💊 Treatment:
It is removed with a minor endoscopic procedure through the nose.

🦴 Why it matters:
An ectopic tooth like this blocks the nose and causes infection and a foul discharge until it is taken out.
generated.ctaText:
If a tooth growing in someone's nose made you do a double take.

I pulled every weird dental and tooth case I have into one PDF.

Impacted teeth, extra teeth, teeth in places they should never be.

Support the page if you'd like and I'd appreciate it 🙏
